description:
# Philosophy

The SKILL.md today *mentions* not modifying files in the main repo — buried in an Important bullet list — but it reads like one rule among many. For the 95% of users who let the bl interface govern their agents end-to-end, this should be the load-bearing premise of the document, not a footnote.

The right framing: balls is the tool, and the correct way to use the tool is that modifications never happen against the operating branch. Worktrees aren't a convenience flag, they are how the system works. An agent that edits files directly on main while "using" balls is misusing it — the task tracker is keeping score of work that happened outside its lifecycle, and the merge/review/close primitives stop being meaningful.

This is slightly looser than my AGENTS.md (which is for my own agents, unconditional), because someone else's agent in someone else's repo isn't mine to govern. But the skill should communicate the philosophy clearly enough that a downstream agent reading SKILL.md walks away understanding: while using balls to track tasks, modifications go through worktrees and merge in. Editing the operating branch directly defeats the tool.

# Why this shape

1. The default-flow section already establishes "one agent finishes the task end-to-end." The natural companion principle is "and the work happens in the worktree, not on main." Both are about the lifecycle being a contract, not a suggestion.

2. Today's wording ("Don't modify files in the main repo while working on a claimed task") is reactive. The replacement should be proactive — frame the worktree as where work belongs, so the question "can I just edit this on main real quick" doesn't even form.

3. The rule should travel with a *why*. Editing on main while a task is claimed produces: untracked drift the worktree doesn't see; merge surprises at bl review time; a delivery commit that doesn't actually contain all the work; ambiguity about what the task delivered.

# Scope

- Promote the worktree principle from a buried bullet to a top-level section near the default-flow section, framed as "how balls works," not "a thing to avoid."
- Reword existing references in the Workflow / Important block to point at the new section instead of restating.
- Tone: declarative and confident ("work happens in the worktree"), slightly looser than AGENTS.md — the principle binds the *balls workflow*, not the user's right to edit their own repo when not using balls.
- Keep the document's existing voice and density. This is a tightening, not a rewrite.

# Out of scope

- No code or binary changes; SKILL.md only.
- Don't add a new Important-style bullet list. Prefer prose.
- Don't duplicate AGENTS.md verbatim — the skill should reflect the philosophy in its own words.

# Done when

- A new or significantly expanded section near the top of SKILL.md frames worktree-isolated work as the way the tool is used.
- Downstream references in Workflow and Important point at it cleanly without restating.
- A reader who skims only the first third of the doc still walks away with the principle.
title: Tighten SKILL.md philosophy: worktree-or-bust on the operating branch
